mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of industrial production acceleration, with a Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 305.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment yields negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit expansion, systematically generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where industrial production drops below the 0.5% threshold.
